# UC-17.1.56 · ISE RADIUS Change-of-Authorization (CoA) Failure Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Security &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When ISE tells a switch 'kick this person off the network' via a special message, we make sure the switch actually heard the message and acted on it.*

---

## Description

Detects RADIUS CoA failures, timeouts, and NAS no-response events at scale. CoA is how ISE pushes runtime authorization changes (quarantine, reauth, port-bounce) to NADs — failures mean the enforcement action didn't reach the device.

## Value

When SOAR pushes a quarantine to ISE and ISE pushes a CoA to the NAD that fails, the endpoint *remains uncontained*. CoA failure detection is the closure validation for incident-response automation.

## Implementation

Confirm *Authentication* category at INFO. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, throttle suppression on `NAS-IP-Address` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Misconfigure CoA shared secret on a lab NAD. Apply ANC quarantine to an endpoint on that NAD. ISE emits 5419 (CoA failed) and the NAD emits %RADIUS-3-COA_NACK. UC-17.1.56 fires.

### Negative scenario

Fix the shared secret. Quarantine succeeds via 5417/5418. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- NAD configured with `aaa server radius dynamic-author` (and matching shared secret).
- `nad_inventory.csv` with `nas_ip`, `coa_supported`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — CoA Health":
- Single value: failures last 1h.
- Top-N NADs.
- Failure-class.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on sustained CoA failure for one NAD.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Shared-secret mismatch** — Realign on NAD and ISE.
- **NAS no response** — UDP/3799 (or 1700) blocked.
- **CoA initiated but NAD doesn't enforce** — NAD has `aaa authorization network ... default group ...` not configured to honour CoA.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5417, 5419, 5420, 5421))
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:ios" "%RADIUS-3-COA")
| eval action = case(MessageCode=="5417", "initiated", MessageCode=="5419", "failed", MessageCode=="5420", "timeout", MessageCode=="5421", "nas_no_response", like(_raw, "%COA_NACK%"), "nas_nack")
| stats count by NAS-IP-Address, action, coa_action
| where action != "initiated" AND count > 5
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: CoA failures last 1h. (2) Top-N NADs by failure count. (3) Failure-class breakdown.

## Known False Positives

- **NAD reload** — During NAD reload, CoA pkts time out; suppress within scheduled change window.
- **CoA shared-secret mismatch** — One-time misconfiguration; permanent failure on that NAD. Triage as config issue, not transient.
- **Older NAD without CoA support** — Some legacy gear doesn't support CoA; tag and exclude.

## References

- [RFC 5176 — Dynamic Authorization Extensions to RADIUS](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc5176/)
- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — RADIUS CoA](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
